title: Grand Convocation Chamber
content:
{{Top|canon=Galactic Senate Chamber}}

{{Quote|The Jedi have ''their'' battles in this war, so have we. Our battlefield is not in space or some distant planet. It is in the Senate Chamber.|[[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]]|<ref name="Republic 61">[[Republic 61|''Republic'' 61]]</ref>}}
The '''Senate Chamber''', also referred to as the '''Great Rotunda''', '''Senate Rotunda''', or '''Convocation Chamber''' and later known as the '''Grand Convocation Chamber''' under the New Republic, was the central chamber of the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]] in the [[Senate District/Legends|Senate District]] on [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The heart of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], it was the very core of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] dating back to [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]. The chamber stood at 100 meters tall and consisted of 1,024 [[Repulsorpod/Legends|repulsorpods]] for the various senatorial delegations that hailed from every corner the galaxy. Ringed by the canyon-like [[Grand Concourse]], the interior of the chamber was bathed in [[Color/Legends|lavender]], a color which was selected by ancient designers as it was the only shade that had not historically been associated with mourning, anger, or war.

In the center of the convocation chamber, a thirty-meter-tall [[Chancellor's Podium/Legends|podium]] sprouted from the floor that was able to lower down into a [[Chancellery Secretariat/Legends|series of offices]] containing the [[Chancellor's working office/Legends|holding office]] of the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], the elected leader of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. On this podium, the Supreme Chancellor would be accompanied by the [[Vice Chair/Legends|Vice Chair]] and the Chancellor's [[Senior Administrative Aide/Legends|Staff Aide]] on the upper level, with the [[Official Reporter/Legends|Senate scribe]], [[Journal Clerk/Legends|journal clerk]], [[Parliamentarian/Legends|parliamentarian]], and [[Sergeant-at-Arms/Legends|Sergeant at Arms]] seated on the lower level.

Several major events took place in this room throughout the history of the Galactic Republic, namely the passage of the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], the [[Military Creation Act/Legends|Military Creation Act]] in [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], and the [[Declaration of a New Order]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Following a refurbishment during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the chamber continued to serve as the seat of the rechristened [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] for two decades before that body's [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolution]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].

After the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberated]] Coruscant during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senate]] of the New Republic occupied the chamber. After a [[Bombing of the Senate Hall|terrorist bombing]] in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], the chamber underwent reconstruction. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Grand Convocation Chamber was converted into the [[Well of the World Brain]] and was the home of the [[World Brain]], a massive [[Yuuzhan Vong]] beast that oversaw the [[Terraforming/Legends|Vongforming]] operations on the world. After the defeat of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], the chamber was once again restored to its original form and served as the seat of the [[Galactic Alliance Senate|Senate]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]].

==History==
[[File:GrandConvocationChamber-TOR.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Grand Convocation Chamber in [[3653 BBY]], during the [[Great Galactic War]].]]

===Galactic Republic===
Erected by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] circa [[3996 BBY]] in the wake of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the Senate Rotunda was built on the remains of the former [[Senate Hall]], which had not been used after the [[Duel in the Senate|duel]] between [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]].<ref name="JMGD" />

At the close of the [[Golden Age of the Old Republic|Republic's Golden Age]], the [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|Vote of No Confidence]] in Chancellor [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] was accepted by the Senate and Senator [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the [[Chommell sector/Legends|Chommell sector]] became Chancellor. During his term, the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] tore the Republic apart; as more worlds broke away, tensions rose. [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Granta Omega]] [[Attack on the Senate Building|attacked the Senate]] during this time, killing several senators with a dangerous [[Zone of Self-Containment|air-borne toxin]] developed by [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]].<ref name="Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard">''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]''</ref>

[[File:Dooku_no_peace_PoP.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], leader of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], addresses the Senate.]]

Eventually the conflict in the Senate evolved into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. As thousands of star systems withdrew from the capital and the Jedi Order suffered calamitous losses in the war, arguments in the Senate chamber became as heated as the Republic's battlefields. After three years of death and destruction in the name of peace, Chancellor Palpatine announced the end of the war and the [[Declaration of a New Order]], the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Disbanding the Jedi Order, Palpatine, in fact the mysterious [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' alter-ego, was confronted by the Order's [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]], [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], in the Convocation Chamber. Hurling over a dozen [[Repulsorpod/Legends|repulsorpods]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and damaging countless others during the [[Skirmish in the Senate|confrontation]], the newly christened [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine fought Yoda to a draw, and the latter fled into exile.<ref name="ROTS" />

===Galactic Empire===
The magnitude of damage in the building's convocation chamber caught the eyes of the Senators. Closing the chamber for repair did not put an end to the spread of rumors that Grand Master Yoda had led an attack on Emperor Palpatine himself, and that the ancient Jedi Master had been killed. After repairs were made the newly appointed [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] began meeting in an advisory fashion, holding what amounted to largely elaborate meetings with the [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]], who would later report back to the Emperor on the result of the deliberation. The Emperor, whose scars prevented frequent public appearances, would use the views of the Senate to temper his own and then act with finality, leaving no room for debate or prolonged discussion.<ref name="Labyrinth of Evil">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref> In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], nearly two decades after the rise of the Empire, the Imperial Senate was [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolved]] after [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] [[Alderaan sector/Legends|sector]] was revealed as a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref> [[1 ABY/Legends|Three years later]], the former Grand Convocation Chamber was used as an [[Opera/Legends|opera]] hall, housing a performance of ''[[The Kallea Cycle]]'', a famous [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaalii]] drama on the life of [[Freia Kallea/Legends|Freia Kallea]] and the founding of the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

===New Republic===
[[File:Bombed_NR_Senate_Hall.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Senate Hall after the bombing by [[Kueller]].]]
Following the death of the Emperor in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], warring factions within the quickly collapsing Empire waged destruction upon Coruscant. The Senate Building, along with the nearby [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], sustained heavy damaged but proved resilient never-the-less. As the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] settled into its role as leader of the galaxy, the Senate Building was repaired and the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] convened with its halls. It was within this room that [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] made his plea before the assembly to allow him to reform the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] and begin searching for new recruits. With a majority backing, Skywalker pledged that the new Order would serve as protectors of the Republic, much as the Jedi had for millennia before the Empire.<ref name="Jedi Search">''[[Jedi Search]]''</ref>

While the structure had proven sound when attacked from the outside in the past, the same could not be said about an internal assault. In [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] during a full session of the Senate, [[Bombing of the Senate Hall|several detonations]] rocked the tower, hurling rubble onto the Plaza outside and collapsing the [[Chancellor's Podium/Legends|central speaking podium]]. After removing the dead and wounded, it was eventually discovered that the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Kueller]] was responsible for the attack.<ref name="TNB">''[[The New Rebellion]]''</ref> After the issue of the bombing was cleared up, the Senate Hall was gutted and new architects swept into the building and reworked the interior, so much so that many considered it a new complex all together.<ref name="SOTP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref>

While the New Republic struggled to establish itself, the galaxy was [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] by an alien race called the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. As attacks against Republic worlds increased, Yuuzhan Vong representative [[Nom Anor]] addressed the Senate and encouraged the assembly to denounce the Jedi and hand them over to the invaders. Then [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Borsk Fey'lya]] rebuffed Anor's plea and had him escorted off Coruscant. However, Anor was successful in increasing the [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jedi sentiment]] among the senators and many Jedi were turned over to the Yuuzhan Vong across the galaxy. As the Senate continued to debate how best to deal with the new threat to the Republic, the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attacked Coruscant]] in force, breaking through its orbital defenses and smashing through into the skylanes of the city world. As mass evacuations ensued following a crushing victory by the invaders, the Senate Building was left relatively intact if abandoned.<ref name="SBS" />

===Well of the World Brain===
Following the fall of Coruscant, the Yuuzhan Vong selected the ruins of the Senate Building to serve as the home of their [[World Brain]], a massive [[dhuryam]] which would transform Coruscant into the legendary homeworld of the invaders, [[Yuuzhan'tar (disambiguation)|Yuuzhan'tar]]. Referred to as [[Well of the World Brain|the Well]] by the Yuuzhan Vong, the Senate Building ruins were hollowed out while the exterior was quickly digested by [[yorik coral]] and hardened into an impenetrable substance. Appearing as a rotting skull from the outside, the Well was further protected by a deadly [[hedge maze]] that was planted over the Senate Plaza. Despite the defenses, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Ganner Rhysode]] were able to enter the Well on the pretense that Solo would consult with the World Brain before sacrificing Rhysode to the True Gods. Within the Well, Jedi Rhysode [[Battle of the World-Well|sacrificed]] himself while protecting Solo as he consulted with the World Brain, convincing it to subtly sabotage the Yuuzhan Vong's [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] of the planet.<ref name="Traitor">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref>

In an effort to retake Coruscant, members of the Jedi Order planned to destroy the World Brain. As Republic heroes [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Solo]] entered the former Atrium with defected Yuuzhan Vong [[Harrar]], a band of [[Shamed Ones]], the lower class of the alien invaders, stormed the building as part of a [[Jeedai heresy|heretical movement]] to overthrow the [[Yuuzhan Vong castes|Yuuzhan Vong caste system]]. Aided by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Kenth Hamner]], [[Mara Jade Skywalker]], and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi]] [[Tahiri Veila]], the Jedi and Republic fighters were able to convince the World Brain to surrender and, through other events elsewhere on the planet, retake Coruscant for the Republic.<ref name="The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

===Galactic Alliance===
With the defeat of the Yuuzhan Vong, the New Republic was reorganized into the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] and, after having met on [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] in the Alliance's formative years, the [[Galactic Alliance Senate|Senate]] returned to Coruscant after the [[Swarm War]] and took up occupancy in the restored Senate Building. Having been stripped of all remnants of the Yuuzhan Vong invasion, the complex was reopened and the Senate quickly went into session discussing the second [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] insurrection with vigor. While debate rang out in the Grand Convocation Chamber, protesters from across the galaxy gathered in the Plaza in support of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]].<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref>

==Layout==
[[File:ITW1_Senate.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A section of the Convocation Chamber's wall, along with the delegation offices beyond.]]
Occupying the majority of the building central mass was the cavernous Grand Convocation Chamber, a tiered chamber sometimes referred to as the Senate Arena. The slightly sloping walls of the funnel-shaped room were lined with [[Repulsorpod/Legends|repulsorpods]] arranged in concentric circular patterns from floor to ceiling, which soared over a hundred meters high. The center of the vast chamber was dominated by a [[Chancellor's Podium/Legends|podium]] used by the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], the [[Vice Chair/Legends|Vice Chair]] and the [[Senior Administrative Aide]] to coordinate the sessions. The chamber contained a total of 1,024 repulsorpods, which could be used by delegations from member systems and large voting blocs. When a Senator or representative wished to address the body they were able to detach their pod from its magnetic berth and move out into the open space of the chamber on an automated path. From there, their address would echo throughout the room, translated into hundreds of different languages instantaneously and piped into the other pods for ease of understanding. [[Senate cam droid/Legends|Small hovercams]] constantly hovered around the chamber, recording the words of the speakers and broadcasting them onto the ceiling-mounted [[Viewscreen/Legends|vidscreens]] to allow the congregation to view up close visuals of the orator. Because sessions tended to grow long and last into the night, natural light and air was not used to fill the room. Instead, an artificial glow kept the room lit so as to diminish the senators' sense of time.<ref name="IWTPM">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars: Episode I]]''</ref>

The main control panel for the chamber's repulsorpods was located on [[Senate Tier 125]]. A smaller version of the Convocation Chamber could be found in the 20-tiered [[Senate Hearing Chamber]], also located within the Senate Building.<ref name=":0">''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]''</ref>

Directly beneath the Convocation Chamber was a series of rooms known as the [[Chancellery Secretariat/Legends|Chancellery Secretariat]], a set of offices used by the Chancellor and his aides while at the Senate Building. At the heart of the Secretariat was the [[Chancellor's working office/Legends|holding office]], located directly below the Senate Chamber and home to the Chancellor's podium when it was not in use. Before the start of a session of the Congress, the Chancellor and his aides would board the podium and the ceiling would dilate and the hydraulics driving the chamber would raise it into arena.<ref name="ROTS" /> A [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] was also installed under the Chancellor's seat, able to evacuate him from the podium without having to lower it.<ref name="Cloak of Deception">''[[Cloak of Deception]]''</ref>

[[File:RepublicFinancialReformBill-POP.png|thumb|left|250px|Senators flood the floor during the vote on the [[Republic financial reform bill/Legends|Republic financial reform bill]].]]

Access to the chamber was granted through a small antechamber where the ceremonial, blue-robed [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guard]] stood at attention. The upper tiers of repulsorpods of the Senate Chamber were each assigned a permanent delegation. These senators represented large voting blocs in the Senate or held long tenures in the Republic, such as the representatives of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. These delegations were afforded permanent wedge-shaped offices, which included a multi-room preparation chamber adjoined to the delegation's platform and a separate, larger set of chambers which stretched to the outer edge of the building and served as a place for each delegation's countless aides to toil endlessly without need of travel.<ref name="IWTPM" />

====Remodeling====
[[File:Declaration.png|thumb|right|250px|The first [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] addresses the Senate from his podium during the [[Declaration of a New Order]].]]
Following the [[Declaration of a New Order]], the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Republic Senate]] was reformed into the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]], which continued to make use of the Senate Building. Gone were statues of the Jedi Order, as were certain privacy buffers in the Senate pods and offices. Many high level Imperials moved their offices to the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] and the number of planets petitioning for admittance into the Empire paled compared with the Republic. Following the highly destructive [[Skirmish in the Senate|duel]] between [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], the Emperor instructed the architects to install an extensive crystal ceiling, replacing the old [[Viewscreen/Legends|holomonitors]] and casting a shimmering light on the central podium. The senior members of the Senate were seated at the lower levels of the chamber during this time, with the newer and less powerful Senators seated in the upper tiers.<ref name="SOTP" />

The Senate Building stood largely unchanged after the fall of the Empire and the occupation of the Building by the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]]. This would change in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] when the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Kueller]] [[Bombing of the Senate Hall|bombed]] the structure, collapsing the Chancellor's podium and ripping out chunks of the chamber and killing many members of the Congress.<ref name="TNB" /> By the time of [[Ponc Gavrisom]]'s term as Chief of State, the building itself was rebuilt, which saw the internal fittings of the auditorium completely replaced, and the Grand Convocation Chamber was thought of by many contemporaries as an entirely "new" structure, distinct from the old rotunda. The chamber was extensively redecorated; the Chief of State's podium was lowered to ground level, and most strikingly of all, in place of the former geometric arrangement of repulsorpods, the seating for delegates was organized into clusters of differing size and shape arranged around the rotunda, connected to each other by ramps and stairways, and capable of holding more than 2,000 delegates. The upper levels were randomly pitted with large chambers to seat other senators or representatives temporarily visiting the planet. It was said that they may have replaced some of the rooms with repulsorlift pods, but by the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]'s taking of the planet, that plan had been long abandoned. The overall effect of the design was to create a complex arrangement of contrasting forms and details, combined nevertheless into a clear, coherent harmony.<ref name="SOTP" /> As membership in the New Republic grew, new balconies were added to the existing clusters to accommodate the growth; rumors about reincorporating the repulsorpods spread during [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s administration, but never came to fruition under the New Republic.<ref name="Traitor" />

Following the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle]] of Coruscant during the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]], the Convocation Chamber was filled with a pool of nutrients and became the home of the giant [[World Brain]], the controlling organ behind the invaders [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] project. Still accessible from the former Atrium of the Senate, a long corridor cut half a kilometer into the building and onto a bridge that spanned half the convocation chamber. The holding office below was transformed into a secret bunker, where a single escape craft was preserved for [[Nom Anor]].<ref name="Traitor" />

Following the defeat of the Yuuzhan Vong, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] restored the Building to its pre-Imperial form. The [[Chief of State's Suite]] was placed at the summit of the building, and repulsorpods were reincorporated into the Grand Convocation Chamber. The Chief's podium was not recreated however; instead they would occupy their own floating platform to make addresses to the Senate.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Senate_HSW.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Grand Convocation Chamber, depicted here during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], a millennium before its actual date of construction.]]

Counter to all prior sources stating that the Grand Convocation Chamber was erected following the [[Duel in the Senate|duel]] in the old [[Senate Hall]] between [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], ''[[Timeline 12: The Great Hyperspace War]]'' depicts the Chamber in operation nearly one thousand years prior to when it should first make an appearance. An explanation for this discrepancy has not yet been offered.
